drm_validate_reml_spec_biv rejects any direct ordinary (non-phylogenetic) random-effect location-SD formula under REML (spec$random_scale$mu$n_models > 0 branch). Use REML = FALSE, or route through the phylogenetic sd_phylo1()/sd_phylo2() formulas instead (admitted, see the phylo direct-SD REML row). Moderate confidence that sd1()/sd2() populate spec$random_scale$mu (the univariate analogue is explicitly sd_mu(group)~predictors; the bivariate cli_abort text names sd_phylo1/sd_phylo2 as the admitted alternative, implying sd1/sd2 are what is being rejected here) -- not independently confirmed by a dedicated test.

drm_validate_reml_spec_biv rejects any direct ordinary (non-phylogenetic) random-effect location-SD formula under REML (spec$random_scale$mu$n_models > 0 branch). Use REML = FALSE, or route through the phylogenetic sd_phylo1()/sd_phylo2() formulas instead (admitted, see the phylo direct-SD REML row). Moderate confidence that sd1()/sd2() populate spec$random_scale$mu (the univariate analogue is explicitly sd_mu(group)~predictors; the bivariate cli_abort text names sd_phylo1/sd_phylo2 as the admitted alternative, implying sd1/sd2 are what is being rejected here) -- not independently confirmed by a dedicated test.

A single unlabelled (1 | id) random intercept on mu1 alone, or on both mu1 and mu2 with no shared covariance-block label, is rejected with a 'covariance-block labels' error. Every biv_gaussian random effect (mu or sigma, intercept or slope) requires an explicit matched label (e.g. (1 | p | id)) spanning >=2 endpoints; there is no independent single-endpoint ordinary RE path in this family. Representative for mu2 and for ordinary_re_slope. Rejected identically under ML and REML (REML never reaches its own gate because the ML-side formula build already errors first).

A labelled sigma1 random intercept with no matching sigma2 (or matching mu) block, or two unlabelled sigma1/sigma2 intercepts with no shared label, is rejected ('matching labelled...mu' / 'covariance-block labels' errors). Representative for sigma2. Rejected identically under ML and REML.

Any '|' bar term in the rho12 formula is unconditionally rejected: 'Residual rho12 is a within-observation correlation, not a group-level random-effect correlation.' rho12 has no ordinary or structured random-effect path at all, matched or not. Rejected identically under ML and REML.

phylo(...) present in mu1 only (mu2 lacking a matching phylo term) is rejected: 'Bivariate phylogenetic location terms must be matched in mu1 and mu2.' Representative for mu2/sigma1/sigma2 analogues at any q-gate size (q1 attempts are rejected before a covariance block is ever built).

spatial(...) present in mu1 only is rejected: 'Bivariate spatial location terms must be matched in mu1 and mu2.' Representative for mu2/sigma1/sigma2 analogues at any q-gate size (q1 attempts are rejected before a covariance block is ever built).

animal(...) present in mu1 only is rejected: 'Bivariate animal location terms must be matched in mu1 and mu2.' Representative for mu2/sigma1/sigma2 analogues at any q-gate size (q1 attempts are rejected before a covariance block is ever built).

relmat(...) present in mu1 only is rejected via the same guard_biv_known_mu_terms() matched-pair check as animal (shared helper, marker='relmat'). Representative for mu2/sigma1/sigma2 analogues at any q-gate size (q1 attempts are rejected before a covariance block is ever built).

phylo_interaction() is one of the five structured_marker_names(), but no guard_biv_phylo_interaction_mu_terms() (or equivalent) exists in drm_build_biv_gaussian_spec -- only phylo/spatial/animal/relmat are stripped and handled. A phylo_interaction() marker in any biv_gaussian dpar formula falls through to the generic drm_reject_phase1_terms() structured-marker message ('Structured-effect syntax is planned, not implemented.'). Representative for mu2/sigma1/sigma2/rho12 and for all q-gates and both estimators.

Arc 1b-S1 admits native-TMB bivariate-Gaussian REML only for the matched labelled location block `spatial(1 | p | site, coords = coords)` in both `mu1` and `mu2`. The coordinate-derived covariance is fixed; each location endpoint has one intercept-only structured member, while `sigma1`, `sigma2`, and `rho12` remain intercept-only; response pairs are complete; weights are one; no known `meta_V()` covariance is supplied; and no additional ordinary random effect, direct-SD formula, or `corpair()` regression is present. An independent dense restricted-likelihood oracle and a predeclared 1,200-attempt Totoro campaign (1,200 fits and convergence-code-zero fits, 1,198 `pdHess`, 9 target-boundary attempts) support point-fit recovery only. This does not admit unlabelled, unmatched, mismatched-label/group/coordinate, multiple-label, slope-only, intercept-plus-slope, predictor-dependent `sigma1`/`sigma2`/`rho12`, q4+, scale-only q2, q2-plus-q2, mesh/range-estimating, incomplete-pair, non-unit-weight, known-`meta_V()`, additional-random-layer, direct-SD, spatial-`corpair()`, random-`rho12`, animal, relmat, non-Gaussian, AI-REML, interval, coverage, inference-ready, or supported claims.

REML rejects any mean-side (mu1/mu2) animal structured term outright, regardless of q-gate (q2/q4/q6/q8/q12) or covariance layout: drm_validate_reml_spec_biv only admits structured_mu_type(phylo_mu) == 'phylo'. Representative across all q-gates and across the mu1/mu2/sigma1/sigma2 members of any such block; set REML = FALSE or use phylo instead.

REML rejects any mean-side (mu1/mu2) relmat structured term outright, regardless of q-gate (q2/q4/q6/q8/q12) or covariance layout: drm_validate_reml_spec_biv only admits structured_mu_type(phylo_mu) == 'phylo'. Representative across all q-gates and across the mu1/mu2/sigma1/sigma2 members of any such block; set REML = FALSE or use phylo instead.

MAJOR-FIX (verifier): scale-only structured q2 block sets phylo_mu$has=TRUE so bivariate REML gate R/drmTMB.R:2056-2113 rejects (no scale_side_only escape, unlike univariate). Representative for animal/relmat scale-only REML too.

Labelled (1 | s | id) sigma1/sigma2 residual-scale q2 block is admitted under REML per drm_validate_reml_spec_biv's inline documentation (beta_sigma1/beta_sigma2 are marginalized in drm_apply_estimator_spec, so the restricted likelihood already adjusts for them); no cli_abort branch in the function rejects it. Evidenced only by an uncommitted scratchpad probe (scratchpad/reml_biv_sigma_re_probe.R, n_id=60), not a merged testthat file -- treat as a lead, not load-bearing evidence, until promoted to tests/.

Matched phylo(1 | p | sp, tree=tree) location block in mu1 and mu2 under REML matches an exact bivariate restricted-likelihood analytic reference (biv_phylo_reml_reference(), stacked-GLS with V = G kron A + R kron I) to tolerance 1e-2 to 5e-2 on sd_phylo1/sd_phylo2/cor_phylo/sigma1/sigma2/rho12/beta. Strong point-estimate accuracy against a known optimum; a sample-size recovery ladder (docs/design/221-native-reml-finish.md) additionally shows bias -> 0 with n and REML/ML SE agreement. No dedicated CI-coverage simulation found for this exact bivariate phylo REML cell, so this stops short of 'supported'.
